methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series C → next-stage step-up multiplier (1.7x) to $105M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 3 same-sector + same-stage peers in Biotech & Health. Range: $1.5B - $4.0B.

methods.name: Public-Comp Anchoring
methods.skip_reason: target revenue not on file (private companies rarely disclose)
methods.rationale: Public Biotech & Health P/S median is 5.8x across 3 tickers. Cannot apply without target's revenue.

public.rationale: Public-co frontier-tech comp in Biotech & Health

private.name: Ultima Genomics
private.rationale: Same sector (Biotech & Health), same stage (Series C), post-money $4B

private.name: Insitro
private.rationale: Same sector (Biotech & Health), same stage (Series C), post-money $2.4B

private.name: Colossal Biosciences
private.rationale: Same sector (Biotech & Health), same stage (Series C), post-money $1.5B

data.narrative:
Aleph Farms is a Series C-stage biotech company that has raised $105M to date. The valuation analysis draws on a comp universe of 3 public-traded biotech peers (RXRX, TEM, ONT.L) and 8 private companies in the Biotech & Health sector, with direct stage-matched comparables including Ultima Genomics ($4.0B), Insitro ($2.4B), and Colossal Biosciences ($1.5B).

Two valuation methods are applicable. Stage-progression analysis applies a 1.7x step-up multiplier typical of Series C advancement to the $105M total raised proxy, implying $178M. Peer-median anchoring across the 3 same-sector, same-stage private comparables yields a median valuation of $2.4B, with a range of $1.5B to $4.0B. Public-comp P/S anchoring cannot be applied because Aleph Farms' revenue is not disclosed; the public median P/S of 5.8x across the 3 biotech tickers is therefore not actionable.

The triangulated analysis of these two applicable methods produces a range of $152M (low) to $2.8B (high), with a median of $1.3B. The wide spread reflects the gap between stage-based methods (which use raised capital as a proxy) and peer valuation (which reflects recent market pricing of comparable companies). Key uncertainties include whether Aleph's technology maturity and commercialization timeline align with the selected peer set, and the impact of sector-wide biotech funding conditions on peer valuation stability.
